seoTitle: How to Create a Scrolling Text Ticker in Power BI
metaDescription: Add a news-style scrolling text ticker to Power BI for live updates, announcements, or key figures that grab attention on any dashboard.
intro: A scrolling text ticker gives Power BI reports a news-style feel, useful for surfacing live updates or key figures without dedicating a whole visual to them. This guide covers setting up and customizing a scrolling ticker so it fits naturally into your report. It's a nice touch for dashboards meant to be viewed continuously, like on an office display.

steps.heading: Set up your Power BI environment
steps.body: Prepare the report page where you want the ticker to appear, keeping in mind it usually works best along the top or bottom edge.

steps.heading: Create your first text ticker
steps.body: Build the scrolling text element using the visual or technique demonstrated, feeding it the text or data you want to display.

steps.heading: Customize the appearance
steps.body: Adjust the scroll speed, font, and color of the ticker so it stays readable and fits the overall look of your dashboard.

steps.heading: Automate real-time updates
steps.body: Connect the ticker's content to a live or frequently refreshed data source so it reflects current information, like updates or announcements.

steps.heading: Apply it to a practical scenario
steps.body: Use the ticker for something like live updates, announcements, or key metrics that benefit from constant visibility.

faq.question: Is a scrolling text ticker a native Power BI feature?
faq.answer: Scrolling tickers typically rely on a specific technique or visual setup rather than a single built-in default feature, so the setup steps matter.

faq.question: Can the ticker update automatically with new data?
faq.answer: Yes, when tied to a data source that refreshes, the ticker content can update automatically alongside the rest of your report.

faq.question: Will a scrolling ticker distract from the rest of my dashboard?
faq.answer: It can if overused, so it's best placed in a fixed area like the top or bottom and kept focused on brief, high-value information.

faq.question: What kind of content works well in a ticker?
faq.answer: Short updates, announcements, or key figures like stock prices or headlines tend to work best in a scrolling ticker format.

faq.question: Is this feature suitable for beginners to set up?
faq.answer: It's approachable for beginners who follow the setup steps closely, though it does take a bit more effort than adding a standard visual.
